title: Knema pulchra

intro: Knema pulchra is a species of tree in the family Myristicaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Knema pulchra include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
